name: Colorado
tooltip: Colorado — Cat 4: No codified statutory right of publicity. Common-law misappropriation may apply.
rightOfPublicity: Common law only. Colorado has not enacted a codified general right-of-publicity statute. Common-law misappropriation may apply — elements vary, but generally: defendant appropriated plaintiff's identity for commercial value, plaintiff is identifiable, defendant received a benefit. Consult an attorney for state-specific application.
postMortem: No codified post-mortem term. Descendibility uncertain at common law.
aiCoverage: C.R.S. § 1-45-111.5 (HB 24-1147, election deepfakes, eff. July 1, 2024). SB 24-205 (Colorado AI Act; effective date now June 30, 2026 after SB25B-004).
remedy: Common-law misappropriation suit. Federal claims available regardless: Lanham Act § 43(a) false endorsement, defamation, and federal NCII statutes apply nationwide.
whatsInteresting: Colorado hasn't built statutory likeness protection. AI-specific statutes exist for narrow areas (election deepfakes, NCII, etc.) but the general right-of-publicity base is common law only.
primarySources: Common-law cases vary by state; consult an attorney licensed in Colorado.
Federal statutes and any state AI-specific statutes listed above.
